parameters.content:
## Hybrid Search on the n8n T&C

Deployed on a self-hosted n8n framework with Qdrant, Ollama.

**How it works**

*   **Data Preparation**: 
     - Uses Qdrant Vector Store node in order to utilize the Recursive Text Splitter 
subnode and generate embeddings of the sample legal document (n8n T&C). 
     - Make use of Qdrant to generate and add sparse vectors (qdrant/bm25 model) 
*   **Query the text to retrieve Qdrant points using hybrid search**

### Key Strengths of This Blueprint
- ***No Overwrite Safety***: By leveraging *updateVectors* on the final node 
instead of a standard upsert, it successfully evades Qdrant's default behavior 
of erasing existing fields during data updates.
- ***Hybrid Search Foundation***: Preparing the database schema using explicit
 dense settings paired side-by-side with an idf modified BM25 sparseVectors 
structure unlocks both semantic meaning and exact keyword indexing capability.
## Test hybrid search by querying the the text

nodes.name: Generate the embeddings of the query

parameters.content:
## Core Workflow Breakdown
**1. Document Extraction & Guardrails (Top Left)**
- *Manual Trigger*: Starts the ingestion process.
- *Read/Write Files from Disk*: Pulls an enterprise terms-and-conditions PDF from a designated directory (/tmp/).
- *Extract from File*: Parses the raw text content out of the PDF file. 
- *Check If Collection Exists & If Nodes*: Queries the Qdrant instance for a collection named "testing". If it doesn't exist, the workflow diverts to the Create Collection node.
- *Create Collection*: Programmatically builds the database infrastructure configured for true hybrid search:
    - Dense Vectors: 768 dimensions using Cosine distance (perfect for nomic-embed-text).
    - Sparse Vectors: A named sparse index ("sparse-text") using Qdrant’s native BM25 model for precise keyword matching.

**2. Advanced AI Vector Ingestion (Top Right)**
- *Merge*: Dynamically reconnects the infrastructure check branch back to the parsed document stream.
- *Qdrant Vector Store (Advanced AI Branch)*: Acts as the data orchestrator feeding the underlying sub-nodes.
- *Default Data Loader*: Takes the parsed text and injects metadata (Document Title and File Name).
- *Recursive Character Text Splitter*: Intelligently chunks the document text to avoid model token limits.
- *Embeddings Ollama*: Runs the local *nomic-embed-text* model to convert the text chunks into dense 768-dimension vectors and pushes them straight into Qdrant.

**3. Sparse Vector Engine & Chat Sync (Bottom Loop)**
- *When Chat Message Received*: An active webhook webhook node waiting for user queries to fire downstream processes.
- *Code Node ("Extract sparse from Qdrant")*: Uses a custom internal HTTP helper to perform a native REST API POST payload call to /points/scroll. It specifically fetches the points while extracting only the "sparse-text" array.
- *Split Out*: Takes the raw array of points returned from Qdrant and splits them into distinct n8n payload items.
- *Aggregate*: Re-combines processed elements into a single uniform structural dataset.
- *Qdrant Update Node*: Executes an updateVectors operation using JSON.stringify($json.data). This ensures that any calculated vector or payload additions map precisely onto existing point IDs without completely overwriting or corrupting the pre-existing dense vector spaces.

main.node: Generate the embeddings of the query
